steps.title: Identify the key comparison needed
steps.content: The claim requires showing that even where nearly 100 percent speak Swahili, many learned it as a non-first language. This demands evidence that total speakers far exceed the 15 million first-language speakers mentioned in the passage.

steps.title: Find the choice that demonstrates the gap
steps.content: Choice A compares Tanzania's 61 million Swahili speakers to the 15 million first-language speakers worldwide, showing a gap that proves most speakers acquired Swahili as a non-first language. Choices B, C, and D either misrepresent the numbers, make unsupported claims, or compare percentages without addressing the language-acquisition issue. That matches choice A — A is correct.
